title: Lines, Containers, Headings, Paragraphs, Lists, Quotes, and Fenced Code · Failure Triage
summary: A preserved build trace contains one plausible source, path, encoding, metadata, parser, renderer, template, URL, asset, accessibility, security, cache, package, deployment, or release defect; locate the first failed boundary before editing. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in line-oriented block parser with explicit continuation rules and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, ca…
objectives: Represent line content, indentation, blankness, offset, tab expansion, newline, and container prefix without discarding source positions.
Apply named block-start and continuation precedence so paragraphs, headings, quotes, lists, thematic breaks, and code resolve consistently.
Parse ATX headings and paragraphs with closing-marker, interruption, inline-content, and empty-heading evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-static-site-python cumulative production static-site generator portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python parser, route, render, graph, and publication models
with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Native filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py,
Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production effects
require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class PublicationPolicy:
    base_path: str = "/handbook/"
    encoding: str = "utf-8"
    raw_html_allowed: bool = False
    max_document_bytes: int = 262_144
    max_nesting_depth: int = 64


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class BuildEvidence:
    revision: str
    content_manifest_digest: str
    route_manifest_digest: str
    artifact_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python static-site generator portfolio ready"

steps.instruction:
Append a runnable pure-Python function headed "# Evidence: ssg-fenced-code-1blxp1" that uses case 4313 to parse headings, paragraphs, containers, and fenced code with explicit line consumption. Keep its defaults runnable, assert an invariant, and return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not claim native filesystem, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py, Jinja, validator, browser, GitHub, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN, cache, or production behavior; verify those boundaries later with Python 3.14.6, CommonMark 0.31.2 fixtures, markdown-it-py 4.2.0, Jinja 3.1.6 strict autoescape, pytest 9.1.1, standards validators, real browsers, clean package installs, reproducible artifacts, staged deployment, rollback, recovery, and release gates.

content.code:
# Evidence: ssg-fenced-code-1blxp1
# Competency: ssg-fenced-code.
# Case 4313: an accessibility-led community newsroom release.
# Operating constraint: preserve source spans, Unicode policy, trust classification, route base, contextual encoding, diagnostics, and accessibility decisions.
# Changed case: replace a trusted author fixture with untrusted raw HTML, unsafe URLs, and an active SVG.
def evidence_ssg_fenced_code_1blxp1(lines: int = 28, consumed_lines: int = 28, blocks: int = 9, invalid_containers: int = 0, unclosed_fences: int = 0) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_1blxp1 = "1blxp1-1pxlb1-4313"
    assert evidence_variant_1blxp1
    evidence_axes_1blxp1 = (
        "reader1blxp1",
        "source4313",
        "parse1pxlb1",
        "route1blxp14313",
        "artifact43131blxp1",
        "recovery1pxlb14313",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_1blxp1)) == 6
    if lines < 1 or consumed_lines < 0:
        return False, consumed_lines, "line-count"
    if consumed_lines != lines:
        return False, consumed_lines, "line-consumption"
    if blocks < 1 or blocks > lines:
        return False, blocks, "block-shape"
    if invalid_containers > 0 or unclosed_fences > 0:
        return False, invalid_containers + unclosed_fences, "block-boundary"
    assert consumed_lines == lines
    return True, blocks, "bounded-block-tree"
